summary: Kinetic energy is the energy of motion, E = ½·m·v². Because it depends on the square of the speed, doubling the speed quadruples the energy.
method: Identify the moving object's mass and speed.
Decide which quantity is unknown — energy, mass, or speed.
Rearrange E = ½·m·v²; solving for speed gives v = √(2E/m).
Watch the square: when you solve for v you take a square root, which has a positive value.
Substitute in SI units (kg, m/s) so the energy comes out in joules.

example.title: Kinetic energy of a sprinter
steps: The unknown is E, so use E = ½·m·v².
